task.question: Using only official Firecrawl docs, what does the Search API say about categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs?
task.agentGoal: Constrain to docs.firecrawl.dev and produce evidence only from that domain.

callRationale.rationale: Started the requested isolated run and inspected the provider schema. Parallel exposed web_search and web_fetch. It did not expose a native domain or source-policy field, so source restriction had to be expressed in the objective and search queries.
Searched with docs.firecrawl.dev-only constraints and targeted queries for Search API categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs.
Fetched the canonical Search API reference and Search feature docs returned by search to get direct excerpts for all four requested fields.

strengths: All visible evidence used in the answer came from docs.firecrawl.dev.
The targeted fetch returned direct Search API/OpenAPI excerpts covering categories, country, timeout, and ignoreInvalidURLs.
The provider output included compact enough excerpts after fetch to support the final answer without needing full_content.
weaknesses: Parallel did not expose a native domain/source-policy control in the tool schema, so the docs.firecrawl.dev restriction depended on query-level site constraints and the objective.
The initial web_search response was verbose and included several related but non-canonical docs pages, making a follow-up fetch necessary.
The harness normalized expectedTermHit metric reported false even though the raw visible excerpts contained the expected terms.

notableFailures.category: direct fix and needs no further changes
notableFailures.failure: No provider-native source/domain filter was available.
notableFailures.fix: Used docs.firecrawl.dev-only instructions in the objective and site:docs.firecrawl.dev constraints in every search query, then only fetched docs.firecrawl.dev URLs.
notableFailures.residualRisk: Query-level constraints are weaker than a native source-policy parameter, but this run's returned and cited evidence stayed on the required domain.

notableFailures.category: direct fix and needs no further changes
notableFailures.failure: The first search result set included useful but broad docs pages and did not make timeout and ignoreInvalidURLs as cleanly answerable as the canonical API reference.
notableFailures.fix: Fetched the Search API reference and Search feature docs with field-specific objective/query focus.
notableFailures.residualRisk: No further call was needed because the fetch returned direct OpenAPI text for timeout and ignoreInvalidURLs.
